# Steam Pak Inventory — Authoritative Asset Catalog

**Captured:** 2026-04-17 (autonomous tick)
**Rule reference:** ENG-1
**Generated by:** zipfile enumeration of Steam baseq3 paks
**Raw data:**
- Full manifest (every file): `steam-pak-manifest-2026-04-17.json` (1.6 MB, 13,358 rows)
- Summary only: `steam-pak-summary-2026-04-17.json` (2 KB)

---

## Headline Numbers

- **13,358 files total** across 10 pak files
- **QL `pak00.pk3`:** 9,285 files (69.5% of corpus)
- **Q3A `pak0.pk3`:** 3,539 files (26.5%)
- **Q3A expansion paks 1-8:** 534 files (4.0%) — mostly bot logic, point-release patches

## Quake Live pak00.pk3 breakdown (9,285 files)

| Category | Count | % | Notes for command center |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| Surface textures | 3,855 | 41.5% | Walls, floors, environment. Primary style-pack target. |
| Skin textures | 1,784 | 19.2% | Player models + weapon skins. High creative value. |
| Sounds | 1,137 | 12.2% | Out of scope v1 (schema ready, tab disabled). |
| UI | 580 | 6.2% | HUD, menus, scoreboard. Cel-shaded pack sweet spot. |
| Models | 512 | 5.5% | Non-weapon, non-player MD3s (ammo boxes, flags, holdables). |
| Texture-other | 398 | 4.3% | Misc `.tga/.jpg/.png` not under textures/. |
| Maps | 299 | 3.2% | `.bsp` + lightmap storage. Map browsing tab in command center uses these. |
| Gfx | 172 | 1.9% | Crosshairs, HUD icons, 2D art. |
| Scripts | 164 | 1.8% | `.shader` files — critical for animated-sprite style packs (Sprint 9 edits these). |
| Bot AI | 148 | 1.6% | `.c` scripts + chat files. |
| Weapons | 148 | 1.6% | MD3 + TGA per weapon × 9 weapons × projectile sprites. THIS is Sprint 6 hero territory. |
| Other | 52 | 0.6% | Config, fonts, etc. |
| Effects | 36 | 0.4% | Explosion / muzzle flash / trail sprites. Sprint 9 animator targets. |

## Quake 3 Arena pak0.pk3 breakdown (3,539 files)

| Category | Count | % | Notes |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| Surface textures | 1,116 | 31.5% | id's 1999 textures. `retro_quake1` and `q2_sonic_mayhem` packs reference these. |
| Skin textures | 793 | 22.4% | Classic Q3 playermodels (Sarge, Xaero, Visor, Doom, Ranger, Keel). |
| Sounds | 622 | 17.6% | — |
| Models | 334 | 9.4% | — |
| Bots | 145 | 4.1% | — |
| Weapons | 110 | 3.1% | Original Q3 weapon set. |
| Maps | 62 | 1.8% | `q3dm1-q3dm19`, `q3tourney*`, etc. |
| Effects | 6 | 0.2% | Minimal — Q3 relied on shader animation more than sprite sequences. |

## Q3A Expansion Pak Roles

| Pak | Files | Purpose |
|---|---:|---|
| pak1 | 26 | v1.17 patch — mostly bot updates |
| pak2 | 148 | v1.27 patch — team arena bot skins added |
| pak3 | 4 | v1.29 patch — minor fixes |
| pak4 | 272 | v1.30 patch — **CPMA bots** and `bots.txt` metadata |
| pak5 | 7 | v1.31 patch — shader fixes |
| pak6 | 64 | v1.32 patch — new effects + 9 new maps (important for map rotation) |
| pak7 | 4 | Point release — misc |
| pak8 | 9 | Point release — UI + misc |

---

## Implications for Command Center Sprint 2 (Asset Browser)

**Inventory ingest strategy confirmed:**
1. For every file in every Steam pak, insert one row into `assets` table with:
   ```
   category    = <script-inferred from path prefix>
   subcategory = <e.g. weapons/rocketl for weapons/rocketl/rocketl.md3>
   source_pk3  = <absolute Steam path>
   internal_path = <path inside the pak>
   checksum    = <sha256 of the inner bytes>
   ```
2. QL assets take precedence — if the same `internal_path` appears in both
   QL `pak00` and Q3A `pak0`, the QL version wins (that's what the engine
   does at alphabetical load order anyway).
3. Expansion paks (1-8) override `pak0` for their files — inventory
   respects the same rule.

**Sprint 6 seed pack decisions confirmed:**
- Weapons inventory (148 QL + 110 Q3A = 258 entries) is small enough
  that Sprint 6 can generate a `photoreal` variant for EVERY weapon
  file and ship a hero pk3 fast.
- Skins (1,784 QL + 793 Q3A = 2,577 entries) are the real creative goldmine
  but too many for a week-one hero ship. Cover 3 priority models
  (Sarge / Xaero / Visor — user's picks from docs/research/existing-assets-inventory).
- Surface textures (3,855 + 1,116 = 4,971 entries) are the largest pool —
  Sprint 7 builds tooling so user can batch-request an entire map's
  textures through one pack.

**Shader files (164 QL scripts) are the animated-sprite pivot point.**
The Sprint 9 sprite animator has to output not just PNG sequences but
also `tcMod` / `rgbGen` shader entries that reference them. That's a
code-gen pass, flagged for spec refinement before Sprint 9 kickoff.

---

## What's NOT in the Steam Paks (we still need wolfcam for these)

- `.dm_73` demo decode tables (live in wolfcamql code, not data)
- WolfcamQL-specific cvars (`wolfcam_*`) — these live in the engine binary
- Our own `phase5/zzz_photorealistic.pk3` (our content, not Valve's)
- Any user-generated `workshop/` content (paths mentioned in `workshop.txt` but user content lives elsewhere)
